district_name: Loma Prieta Joint Union School District

goals.description:
All students will demonstrate upward mobility in meeting or exceeding mathematics and English language arts standards,
as measured by state (SBAC) and local assessments. (LCAP State Priority #1, #2, #4 and #7)

goals.description:
English Learner (EL) students will make progress of at least one level on the summative CA English Language Proficiency
Assessment (ELPAC). (LCAP State Priority #2, #3, and #4)

Proficiency
Assessment

data: 53.3% of
students increased at

actions.title:
Course
access/instructional
materilals
actions.description:
Ensure that every pupil in the school district has sufficient access to
standards-aligned instructional materials based on the most recent state
frameworks. Maintain student access to Algebra, Geometry, lab science,
and/or STEM. Continue to refresh student devices to ensure digital
access.

actions.title:
Standards-based
reporting
actions.description:
Vertically align K-8 ELA and math curriculums so students are prepared for
high school. Modify elementary report cards to be standards-based and tie
in with local district-wide assessments.
This action is part of regular staff responsibilities therefore there are no
additional expenditures.

actions.title:
Math intervention
support
actions.description:
Provide research-based math intervention program (K-8) that provides
frequent progress monitoring.

actions.title:
Reading intervention
support
actions.description:
Provide research-based reading intervention program (K-8) that provides
frequent progress monitoring.

actions.title:
Targeted Math &
Reading supports
actions.description:
Additional 0.2FTE (math) and 0.4FTE (reading) targeted to support SED
struggling math and reading students

goals.description:
Pupil Engagement - Students will engage on a regular and consistent basis with curricular and
extracurricular materials and activities.

actions.title: Attendance Intiatives
actions.description:
P r o vide SART and SARB meetings as need arises for students identified
as struggling with daily attendance. Purchase Personalized Learning
Instruction (PLI) from iReady as a means to assist with Independent Study
options so that our district can improve attendance for all students and
specifically SWD, SED, Hispanic, and White student groups.
iReady expenditure costs are under different goals and actions

actions.title:
Student-Led
Organizations
actions.description:
Provide more opportunities for students to connect and participate in
student-led groups (i.e. Leos, Club 41, Buddies, Peer-tutoring, etc.)
This action is part of regular staff responsibilities therefore there are no
additional expenditures.

actions.title:
School-based
counseling/support
services
actions.description:
Provide school-based counseling and support services for all students.
Provide additional services to our Hispanic, White, SED, and SWD
students struggling with attendance issues.
Counseling and support service expenditure costs are under different goals
and actions

actions.title: Translation Services
actions.description:
P r ovide annual translation phone line to allow staff and parents to
effectively communicate with our non-English speaking parents. Utilize
PowerSchool translation module to convert enrollment documentation.
Offer Community Liaison services to support and navigate our EL families.

actions.title:
Targeted counseling
for attendance
actions.description:
Counseling services for students identified as at risk for chronic
absenteeism.

actions.title:
Targeted
Transportation
Services
actions.description:
Provide bus passes for EL & SED students to assist with improving
attendance

goals.description:
School Climate - Students will feel physically and mentally safe and secure at school. In addition,
parents/guardians will feel supported and appreciated at our schools while engaging in our school
activities and events.

actions.title:
Alternatives to
suspension
actions.description:
Develop & implement alternatives to suspensions and offer restorative
practices to reduce the number of annual suspensions per student,
especially for our SWD students.
This action is part of regular staff responsibilities therefore there are no
additional expenditures.

actions.title: Loma Family System
actions.description:
C o ntinue to provide ways to include all students in our Loma Family
System so that each student feels more connected to our district and to
encourage cross-age relationships.
This action is part of regular staff responsibilities therefore there are no
additional expenditures.

actions.title:
Student Wellness
Strategies

Parent/Community
Communication
actions.description:
Provide multiple avenues for one & two way communication with parents &
community: Maintain webpages (Apptegy); Use ThrillShare to push out
social media posts and emergency communications; Provide quarterly
Parent Ed & Special Ed nights; Offer monthly Board Chats; Send out
weekly newsletters.

actions.title:
Behavioral support
staff
actions.description:
Provide for a Special Education counselor; Behavioral Specialist, and
Safety Care trained para-educators to support and reduce SWD discipline
and suspensions.

actions.title:
Student behavior
management/recogni
tion programs
actions.description:
Reinforce positive student behaviors through district-wide behavior
management procedures and recognition activities.
This action is part of regular staff responsibilities therefore there are no
additional expenditures.

actions.title:
Student orientation
programs
actions.description:
Conduct orientation programs to support seamless transitions between
elementary, middle, and high school to increase school climate on campus.
This action is part of regular staff responsibilities therefore there are no
additional expenditures.

actions.title:
Improve safety on
campus

goals.description:
Resources - Students will have access to the best staff and safest, healthiest, up-to-date facilities
that promote intellectual growth and well-being

actions.title:
Teacher
development
programs
actions.description:
Support teacher induction programs focusing on credentialing
requirements and mentoring program; Provide effective professional
development; Ensure all staff is highly qualified.

actions.title:
Improve technology
access
actions.description:
Continue to provide support for the effective integration of instructional
technology; Update IT infrastructure; Continue refresh cycle on student
devices; improve phone access; replace bells, clocks, notification system.

actions.title:
Monitor annual safety
plans
Improve facilities
actions.description:
In order to ensure our staff and students have access to the best facilities
and resources, we will focus to complete Phase 2 Bond projects and begin
work on Phase 3 bond projects.
